title: Why label the New Movies as fanfiction
display_title: Why label the New Movies as fanfiction
wikitext:
[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 18, 2010 8:27 pm Enki v.2 wrote: I'm subscribing to the "Rebuild is Anno's evangelion fanfiction" theory.

[]: Post by Tails_night » Sun Jul 18, 2010 8:30 pm subAtomic wrote: What is datenroku and where can I listen to Kaworu's speech?

[]: Post by Enki v.2 » Sun Jul 18, 2010 8:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Also, despite the fact it's more or less the same staff, the studio that's working on the new movies is kara, not Gainax.

[]: Post by esselfortium » Sun Jul 18, 2010 8:35 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Also, despite the fact it's more or less the same staff, the studio that's working on the new movies is kara, not Gainax.

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:03 pm It certainly feels like the stuff a fan would create. (Horrible nonsense)

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:04 pm That we have a thread dedicated to a single specific insult directed towards the films, their creators, and the fans who support them is pretty absurd. That people are getting defensive and actually claiming to be offended that one of their many pet insults is being itself insulted and disrespected is the height of absurdity.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:09 pm In the strictest sense of the word--which is what I'm commenting on--the "fanfiction" claim is ridiculous. You don't have to like the new films, but calling something fanfiction when its a work by the original creator(s) is discrediting (and an insult) to the fact it's still by THEM. Besides, the new films are an alternate retelling which means it has no bearing on NGE canon. NGE and NME are separate universes by the original creator(s), what happens in one of these universes won't have bearing on the other. And, Essel , I'm not sure why you're personally insulted over an image when it wasn't even addressed to you. Since it wasn't obvious, the image was a joke and something I created awhile ago for the fanart thread . Enki : People change, nobody ever said they don't. My point is that strictly speaking, something is only fanfiction when it's NOT the original creator(s) working on something. (Also, Anno commented on Eva being "an interesting show" .) All of these labels when it comes to the new movies--"fanfiction", "butthurt troll", whatever--are just silly and continuously take away from proper, civil, discussion. EDIT: No, I didn't lock this thread.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 14, 2010 12:53 pm Things were slightly heated, but let's give this thread another go provided people stay civil. I already said my two cents on the matter but if anybody else wants to debate this, feel free.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 14, 2010 1:59 pm Enki v.2 wrote: Why should fanfiction be limited to people who didn't create the original?

[]: Post by Oz » Sat Aug 14, 2010 2:40 pm It seems to me that the majority of people writing in this topic have forgotten the difference between sentences "Rebuild is a fanfic" and "Rebuild is like a fanfic".

[]: Post by oOoOoOo » Sat Aug 14, 2010 3:07 pm Tines-san that was a very interesting post. Thanks for sharing that, especially the Picasso bit. It is worth noting that "fanfiction" is a very recent concept. Historically, so many stories have been reimaginings of existing stories. What does it mean to say Rebuild is "like a fanfic"? I know I've made this joke before, but I no longer feel comfortable saying it. As Tines-san notes, Anno-san is a different person today. Mental illness is very much about perspective, and he's going to view the world differently now that he is relatively happy. We also have to consider his intentions. Let us assume his goal is to create a more upbeat, action-oriented Evangelion. Inside his head exists all of the ideas he's ever had about the franchise. We do not know how much of Rebuild predates the original series. When he was creating the original series, presumably there were many "what if" situations that he discarded, which may be emerging now. What we call "canon" happens to be ideas that were lucky enough not to be discarded, but that doesn't mean the other ideas never existed. Considering my mental state, if I were to direct Rebuild, it would probably be closer to the original series in tone, if not more fucked up and bizarre. Someone might argue "Ophelia understands the true vision of Evangelion!" but really I'd just be reacting to what came before, and infusing my own ideas. Anno-san isn't reacting to what came before, so much as he is continuing the thought process. I doubt a day has gone by where he hasn't thought about Evangelion. It is important to consider this. For what it is worth, I predict I'll probably always like NGE better than Rebuild.

[]: Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 14, 2010 3:47 pm I'm going to take the opposite route and say it's really not like a fanfic. A fanfic is the work of an impassioned admirer of a story who is driven to fix what they believe holds the show back from achieving perfection, to simply have more of it, or to combine it with one or more of their other fandoms to attain a higher state of fangasm then either could achieve on thier own. Regardless of the quality of the results, it's a work of individual will. In some ways the original NGE is like a fanfic of the entire robot anime genre, complete with author-insert charecter Shinji. Rebuild, on the other hand, feels much less impassioned and individualistic. Rather, I'd say Rebuild feels much more like a "written in the boardroom" corporate money grab. Thus Toji is replaced in the pilot roster by a doujin-friendly newcomer and we get a smorgasboard of new plugsuit and eva variants to keep the toy manufacturers happy. So what if the story and characterization suffers as long as the action scenes and over-the-top dramatics keep butts in seats on the money train?

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:09 pm Who really gives a shit? The old Evangelion is still there for us all to watch any time we want, and it's a bit unfair to be expecting something both totally original and as heartfelt and masterful AND DIVINELY PERFECT AS TEH ORIGINAL in the form of four movies. Come on, now. It's always been intended to be fucking fanservice to wet our whistles and reward us with cool shit. Just roll with it and stop being ungrateful dicks.

[]: Post by Lucretius » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:13 pm I don't expect "divine perfection from the movies"; I'd settle for a coherent story that can stand on its own. Rebuild relies on the TV show the way Family Guy relies on dates pop culture references.

[]: Post by Taekmkm » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:40 pm It's a coherent story. No newbies I've shown the movie to lost track of the story or characters. As much coherent as episode 1-19 would be if you stopped there.

[]: Post by Lucretius » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:47 pm I meant "incoherent" as in "the characters follow contrived arcs that make little dramatic sense and have little emotional resonance." You'd have to be pretty thick not to follow the robots vs. giant monsters storyline.

[]: Post by Warren Peace » Sat Aug 14, 2010 5:04 pm It's ludicrous to call Rebuild "fan fiction". The orginal creator can do anything he wants with his creation, including re-tell it. It's a silly putdown based on nothing. Lucretius wrote: I don't expect "divine perfection from the movies"; I'd settle for a coherent story that can stand on its own. Rebuild relies on the TV show the way Family Guy relies on dates pop culture references.

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Sat Aug 14, 2010 5:18 pm I wonder how much things have changed since the original statement regarding Rebuild. Many different desires are motivating us to create the new "Evangelion" film. The desire to portray my sincere feelings on film. The desire to share, with an audience, the embodiment of image, the diversity of expressions, and the detailed portrayal of emotions that animation offers. The desire to connect today's exhausted Japanese animation [industry] to the future. The desire to fight the continuing trend of stagnation in anime. The desire to support the strength of heart that exists in the world. Finally, the desire to have these wishes be realized. For these purposes, we used the best methods available to us to make another Evangelion film. Many times we wondered, "It's a title that's more than 10 years old. Why now?" "Eva is too old", we felt. However, over the past 12 years, there has been no anime newer than Eva. Specifically, among the stagnant mood of the present day, it is the portrayal of will - not technology - that is most important. To support the fans that support animation, we felt that a work that would appeal to middle and high school-aged men, who quickly grow away from Anime, was necessary. When we decided that we wanted to something to support the anime [industry] of today, the determination to return to this title was strong. As the creator of this project, [I assure you that] a very new-feeling Evangelion world has been constructed. For this purpose, we are not returning to our roots at Gainax. I have set up a production company and studio, and it is in this new setting that we will start again. Without looking back, without admiration for the circumstances, we aim to walk towards the future. Thankfully, we have gathered staff from the old series, new staff, and many other fantastic staff to work on this series. We realize that we are creating something that will be better than the last series. ”Eva" is a story that repeats. It is a story where the main character witnesses many horrors with his own eyes, but still tries to stand up again. It is a story of will; a story of moving forward, if only just a little. It is a story of fear, where someone who must face indefinite solitude fears reaching out to others, but still wants to try. We hope that you look forward to the 4 new retellings of this story. In closing, it is also our job to provide a service to our customers. Although it seems obvious, we aim to create a form of entertainment that anyone can look forward to; one that people who have never seen Evangelion can easily adjust to , one that can engage audiences as a movie for theatres, and one that produces a new understanding of the world. This fall, we hope you can join us. Creator/General Director, Anno Hideaki

[]: Post by slothen » Sat Aug 14, 2010 5:23 pm i lol at dis thread.

[]: Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Aug 14, 2010 5:29 pm Nonoriri wrote: I wonder how much things have changed since the original statement regarding Rebuild. If it isn't better than the original, if it isn't even comparable, why does it exist?
